scenario: Helios SaaS runs a tenant provisioning service on Camunda 8.8 that handles 25000 requests per day. The platform team is upgrading the underlying framework to Spring Boot 4.0 to align with a wider company initiative and must pick the supported Camunda Spring Boot Starter artifact for that target.
question: Which Camunda Spring Boot Starter artifact provides Spring Boot 4.0 support on Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Use camunda-spring-boot-starter on version 8.8.x which bundles Spring Boot 3.5.x and already covers Spring Boot 4.0 on the same JDK 17 baseline
Use camunda-spring-boot-3-starter from version 8.8.15 which is the dedicated artifact added for Spring Boot 4.0 support across all 8.8 patches
Use camunda-spring-boot-4-starter from version 8.8.9 which is the dedicated artifact that bundles Spring Boot 4.0.x on JDK 17 or higher
Use camunda-spring-boot-4-starter on version 8.8.x which is the dedicated artifact that bundles Spring Boot 3.5.x on JDK 17 or higher

a.text: Incorrect. The default camunda-spring-boot-starter on 8.8.x stays on Spring Boot 3.5.x for all 8.8 patch releases; Spring Boot 4.0 support is provided by a separate artifact.

b.text: Incorrect. camunda-spring-boot-3-starter is an alias for the default starter that keeps Spring Boot 3 when upgrading to 8.9 or newer; it is not the Spring Boot 4.0 artifact.

c.text: Correct. Starting with the Camunda 8.8.9 patch release a dedicated starter artifact, camunda-spring-boot-4-starter, was added that bundles Spring Boot 4.0.x and requires JDK 17 or higher.

d.text: Incorrect. camunda-spring-boot-4-starter bundles Spring Boot 4.0.x, not 3.5.x, and is available from the 8.8.9 patch onward as a drop-in replacement.

explanation: On Camunda 8.8 the Spring Boot 4.0 path is camunda-spring-boot-4-starter, introduced with the 8.8.9 patch release and bundling Spring Boot 4.0.x on JDK 17 or higher. The default camunda-spring-boot-starter remains on Spring Boot 3.5.x across all 8.8 patches.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter version compatibility
